channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Food recycling bins not collected, 19th June
item.description:
Our food bins were not collected this week
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stamford Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Contractor detritus, 19th June
item.description:
There are numerous plastic road barriers, removed traffic island steel frames, a removed lamp post, a dislodged wooden 'parking sign [dislodged to enable contractor to park on the grass verge], discarded road material, plus black bin bags of rubbish, which have left UNCOLLECTED by the inept Contractor for more than two weeks now
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Worsley Bridge Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked roadside rainwater drain causing flooding / overflow during heavy rain, 19th June
item.description:
This roadside rainwater gully/drain has been partially blocked with compacted soil, silt, leaves and debris for at least the last couple of years and no longer drains properly. Because the road slopes towards this point, significant volumes of rainwater collect here during heavy rainfall. As the drain is obstructed, water cannot enter the gully efficiently and instead overflows, causing pooling/flooding along the kerb and carriageway. The blockage appears to be both on the surface (visible debris and silt around the grate) and likely within the gully itself, as water remains slow to clear even after rainfall. This creates a drainage issue and increases the risk of standing water, splashing onto pedestrians/property, and accelerated road surface deterioration. The drain requires inspection and clearance (jetting/gully cleansing) to restore proper drainage capacity.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lakedale Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping, 19th June
item.description:
Fly tipping (old wooden pallet) on the corner of Liskeard Gardens / Kidbrooke Gardens

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kidbrooke Gardens, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped tree cuttings from marston court, 19th June
item.description:
Large tree cuttings have been dumped from resident of marston court SIDCUP in the entrance to Carlton fields SIDCUP . It is blocking the path which runs from the crescent to the field. Can these be removed please
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Crescent, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped on the crescent nr dog waste bin, 19th June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Crescent, Longlands, Greater London

item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Traffic lights stuck on red for extended periods – Treacle Mine Roundabout, 19th June
item.description:
The traffic lights at Treacle Mine Roundabout are staying on red for an unusually long period when approaching from Lodge Lane. This is causing significant delays and traffic build-up, particularly during busy periods. Vehicles are queueing excessively, and it is impacting overall traffic flow through the roundabout. The issue appears to be ongoing rather than a one-off occurrence, suggesting there may be a fault with the signal timings or equipment. Location details: Treacle Mine Roundabout – approach from Lodge Lane Impact: Long queues and congestion Delays for commuters Potential driver frustration and unsafe behaviour Request: Please investigate the traffic light timings or any potential fault and resolve as soon as possible.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lodge Lane, Stifford Clays, Grays
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous bowing wall on corner house will collapse, 19th June
item.description:
Resident reporting the wall on the corner house of Hook Lane going into Cumberland Avenue is bowing and can fall at any time onto the pavement and hurt children or animals, some bricks are already missing and this needs urgent barriers around the Pavement. Resident reporting the wall can collapse at any time. Please could someone look into this asap
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cumberland Avenue, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement impassable in wheelchair, 19th June
item.description:
Bushes and trees encroaching on the pavement along the whole road. Can’t pass in a wheelchair

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Holmbury View, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The bin next to the "derwent rd" bus stop has gone missing, 19th June
item.description:
There used to be a bin by this bus stop, but its not there any more. there is lots of rubbish piling up around the bus stop, and on the grass next to the bus stop.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Anerley, Greater London

item.title: 3 big potholes in the road, 19th June
item.description:

item.title: Very tall & thick tree needs pruning branches hanging down, 19th June
item.description:
Resident reporting the Tree o/s her property is a very thick and very tall tree, the branches hang low and blocks the view of house opposites roof this is also blocking the light and shows how low the branches are hanging. Please could someone look into this
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park Crescent, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
